commit.message:
fix: create collection before cloneOrPull so import never loses the collection

The previous fix moved collection creation before the scan loop, but
cloneOrPull was still the first line in discoverAndImport. If the clone
failed for any reason (credentials, network, git not in PATH), the
collection INSERT was never reached.

Fix:
- Create/find the collection as the very first step, before any network
  calls, so it's always persisted to disk regardless of clone outcome
- IPC handler now recovers gracefully: if discoverAndImport throws after
  the collection was written, we look it up and return it anyway so the
  UI can still navigate to the (empty) collection

commit.message:
feat: configurable colour palette via `flyline set-color` (#385)

Users had no way to configure flyline's UI colours. This adds a
`set-color` subcommand backed by the
[`parse-style`](https://crates.io/crates/parse-style) crate for
rich-syntax style strings.

## Changes

- **`settings.rs`** — `ColorPalette` struct holding `inline_suggestion`
and `matching_char` styles, with `dark()` (original) and `light()`
presets. Added as `color_palette` field on `Settings`.
- **`palette.rs`** — `static ACTIVE_PALETTE:
LazyLock<RwLock<ColorPalette>>` global; `set_active_palette()` updates
it. `matched_character()` and `history_inline_meta()` now read from the
global — no call-site changes required.
- **`lib.rs`** — `SetColor` subcommand + `ColorDefault` enum.
`parse_style()` manually converts `parse_style::Style` →
`ratatui::style::Style` (the project's custom ratatui fork uses
`ratatui-core 0.1.0-beta.0`, incompatible with `parse-style`'s crates.io
`ratatui-core 0.1.0` feature). Invalid style strings print an error and
return a usage exit code.
- **`README.md`** — help block updated; new "Colour palette" section
with syntax and examples.

## Usage

```bash
flyline set-color --default dark           # original palette (default)
flyline set-color --default light          # preset for light terminals
flyline set-color --inline-suggestion "dim italic"
flyline set-color --matching-char "bold green"
flyline set-color --matching-char "#ff8700"        # RGB hex
flyline set-color --default light --matching-char "bold blue"
```

Style strings follow [rich
syntax](https://rich.readthedocs.io/en/stable/style.html):
space-separated attributes (`bold`, `dim`, `italic`, `underline`,
`reverse`, `strike`, …) and colours by name, `color(N)`, `#rrggbb`, or
`rgb(r,g,b)`.

commit.message:
Allow FlowerRecipe to pass extra_env with test (#4322)

### Description
The Flower integration initializes an env dict inside `FlowerRecipe` but
currently ignores the `extra_env` parameter. This makes it impossible to
inject custom environment variables into the Flower job. This PR changes
`FlowerRecipe` to start from `extra_env` (if provided) and then set
`CLIENT_API_TYPE_KEY` on top. This keeps the required external client
API setting while allowing users to pass additional environment
variables through `extra_env`. Added
`test_flower_recipe_merges_extra_env` to
`tests/unit_test/recipe/flower_recipe_test.py` to verify that
`extra_env` entries are preserved and `CLIENT_API_TYPE_KEY` is set.

Refactor SKManagedStream: replace parent/child chain with SKData snapshot (#3589)

Replace the fragile parent/child weak-reference ownership chain in
SKManagedStream with a lazy SKData snapshot approach for duplicate/fork.

OnDuplicate() now creates a native SkMemoryStream backed by a cached,
ref-counted SKData snapshot of the .NET stream. OnFork() duplicates and
seeks to the current position. The original stream remains fully readable
after duplication, multiple duplicates are allowed, and each duplicate is
an independent native stream with no managed interop overhead.

This is both simpler and faster than the old approach:
- No parent/child weak references or ownership transfer logic
- No VerifyOriginal() / InvalidOperationException on the original stream
- Snapshot is cached (one read, shared by all duplicates)
- Native SkMemoryStream duplicates expose getMemoryBase(), enabling
  zero-copy fast paths in Skia (e.g. CoreText font loading on macOS)

Tests updated to reflect new semantics:
- Removed SkipOnNonWindows() from 6 tests (no longer throws in native
  delegates, so all platforms can run these tests now)
- Added NonSeekableStreamDuplicateAndForkReturnNull test
- Renamed DotNetStreamIsClosedWhenSKManagedStreamIsDisposed to document
  that the .NET stream is closed when the parent is disposed (the
  snapshot keeps duplicates alive independently via native ref-counting)

Based on the original SKManagedStream refactoring from PR #3560.

commit.message:
fix: write class_names.txt and embed class_names in args for deploy_to_roboflow (#890)

The Roboflow upload pipeline requires either a class_names.txt file in
the upload directory or class_names in the checkpoint args namespace.
After switching to PyTorch Lightning training, class_names was no longer
embedded in the args namespace, causing RFDETRSeg (and any model) uploads
to fail with FileNotFoundError from the Roboflow client library.

Fixes: deploy_to_roboflow now writes class_names.txt and populates args.class_names before saving the checkpoint.

Adds regression tests covering:
- class_names.txt written with correct one-per-line content
- args.class_names set when absent (the regression scenario)
- existing args.class_names not overwritten
- temp directory cleaned up after successful deploy

* fix: try/finally cleanup + fix broken deploy tests
* fix: accept **kwargs in deploy_side_effect for forward-compat
* fix: write class_names.txt with explicit utf-8 and LF newlines
* test: cover args.class_names is None branch in deploy_to_roboflow
* test: update _make_mock_self default size to 'rfdetr-small'
* fix: widen version param type to int | str in deploy_to_roboflow

commit.message:
feat: update order, user, and vendor services

- Enhanced payment service integration in order-service
- Updated controllers, routes, and services across all three microservices
- Added new dependencies to order-service
- Improved API consistency and error handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Detect Copilot CLI crash and recover with retry button

When the CLI process dies mid-stream, the app previously hung indefinitely
on 'Generating...' with no way to recover.

Detection:
- CopilotService subscribes to the SDK's internal Process.Exited and
  JsonRpc.Disconnected events (via reflection into _connectionTask.Result)
- Process.Exited fires instantly on process kill; JsonRpc.Disconnected
  fires on RPC transport breaks (RemotePartyTerminated)
- Both feed into a single CliProcessExited event with generation-based
  deduplication (Interlocked.CompareExchange)
- Old handlers are unsubscribed on reconnect to prevent leaks

Recovery (SessionLifecycle):
- OnCliProcessExited cleans up streaming/reasoning state, hides typing
  indicator, closes tool groups
- Shows a single error message with a 'Try again' button
- SessionErrorEvent handler skips cleanup if crash handler already ran
  (cliExitHandled flag checked on UI thread)
- Auto-reconnects to new CLI process in background

Retry button:
- Sends 'Try again' as a hidden prompt (no user message bubble) via
  _silentRetryPrompt flag checked in SendMessage
- Button hides itself on click to prevent spam
- try-catch in RetryAfterConnectionLossAsync clears flag on failure

Also adds StreamJsonRpc as a direct PackageReference (was transitive from
the SDK) for strongly-typed Disconnected event subscription.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
